reviews.review_text: Subnautica with a dash of satisfactory. it’s so incredibly satisfying to watch the progression, anyone who likes survival crafting games will love this game, I assure you
After 774 hours of play, I still love the game. There are so many options, recipes,  exploration, harvesting, planning automatic chores, it's never dull

Certainly one of the video games of all time. The Planet Crafter's main appeal is the unique aspect of the world around you visibly changing as the game progresses. Outside of this, The Planet Crafter does nothing excellent. Base-building is particularly frustrating, and exploration becomes more stale the further in you get, eventually reaching a point where every building contains the same items, give or take a couple of vanity blueprints. Every new tier of upgrades in the game is substantially better than the last, giving players zero reason to focus on anything but the newest unlock. And ultimately, The Planet Crafter still has a few large chunks of waiting around even when you've done everything that you can do progression-wise. Due to these things, The Planet Crafter oftentimes felt more like an idle game than a sandbox. Which is fine. The entirety of the game works as it's intended to, and I think that considering the very small size of the team that made this game, they should be proud of what they were able to do. The game is very fine, and very fine games turn into good games the moment they let you bring friends along.

The multiplayer aspect elevates The Planet Crafter from something I would have spent 2 hours on and then uninstalled into something I fully completed with my friend. The Planet Crafter will last you longer than your typical friendslop game and won't eat your savings away to purchase it. If you're looking for a chill time to play a very decent progression-based game with your buddies, The Planet Crafter is an excellent option.

An amazing game to buy if you want a chill experience with beautiful scenery that looks even better once you’ve worked for it. I’m shocked by how much content and effort has been put into this game, especially for a Unity game. Updates keep coming out left and right, and the developer is really responsive when it comes to fixing bugs and listening to feedback.

Highly recommended.
A very relaxing game, no monsters, no battles, just planning and exploration.

Just straight off the bat, this is one of the best games I've ever played. While not as intense as Subnautica or Satisfactory, the game provides the player with a great sense of awe and wonder as you explore and terraform the planets you're stuck on. This is especially true on the first and biggest planet where you uncover hidden lore in secret locations and see your terraforming efforts show visible results in real time. There's base building, farming, animal genetic manipulation, portal hopping, bee keeping, mining, drone management (you can automate a lot of this game at a certain point), trading, crashed wreck scavenging, spelunking, three possible endings, etc. Like this game has almost everything you could ever want. And with a friend, or up to ten players which is actually insane, this game just gets better. It's the definition of simple fun for myself and thousands of other people. Buy this right now for full price including the two dlcs. I cannot recommend this game enough.
It's a fun game. You start with practically nothing on a dusty musty funky planet, and make it pretty. Like the planet before humans :D

this game... OBSESSED! i love that they had a demo to try and while playing the demo i was just like, yup, take my dam money, and brought the full game
I didnt buy the DLCs but i 100% will. i play this alone and its such a chill game to play, it might be fun with others but i dont want anyone coming in and messing up my world ! like GET OUT! 
you will get lost in this game, hours will pass, children will go hungry, partners unloved. 

got to go, i didnt read the opening message about building in high altitude and now my base is starting to drown ! byeeeeeeeee :)

Finally leaving a review after completing all achievements while working on a multi-planet save. If you couldn't tell by my hours played, I love this game. There are periods of the game that are a bit tedious (especially if it's not your first playthrough), but there are stories imbedded in the worlds to encourage exploring during these periods or you can focus on base building/automation. I do wish that the later planets/DLC had achievements as well, because when I finish this run (1.5 planets left to go), I'll probably have to take a break until either more is added or I rope someone into playing multiplayer. The base game itself is great, and I highly recommend getting the DLCs as well.
super addicting game. leans more on the cozy side than survival side (although that oxygen level will get you early game if you're not paying attention - rip me)

An absolute gem of a video game. I don't even like this genre and I was still enthralled from the beginning to the end. Exploration, crafting, building, mysteries, story telling, it has everything packaged in a delicious whole. I can't recommend it enough and I can't wait to play with my kids after they've grown up.
Very very good sandbox terraforming game, gives off major Subnautica vibes. Would totally recommend it if you like survival sandboxes. 10/10

Don't let the Demo fool you, this game actually paces really well and you do actually feel like you are making meaningful progress.

It's also absolutely amazing seeing the planet change in real time. At different milestones you'll notice tangible changes in terrain and areas not just the sky, it is such a enjoyable experience that makes some of the more monotoinous moments feel worth it in a way that the demo really doesn't do justice.

If you like games that give you tangible changes as you progress this game will feel right at home for you.
Fun and in some respects, educational. Sometimes its nice to have a game that doesn't involve killing everything in sight.
When you understand everything, the game becomes fun, but there is no real challenge or fear of death

The game has perfect gameplay. This is a true spirit of Robinson Crusoe, you land on a barren rock and transform the land around. This is a unique feature that so many open world survival craft games lack - they usually have a static map. I cant convey to you with words how great it feels to see the planet become alive, you need to experience that yourself. 

but the graphics do not look good. The interface sucks ass, borderline unusable.

I know there are some that may hate on the graphics but so far I absolutely love this game. I love that there are other planets besides the first to explore! I love the terraforming and how new areas get unlocked. It's incredible!

I can see the appeal and downsides to Planet Crafter, and I enjoyed it, to a point.
This game really tickled my gaming "lizard brain" collecting the shiny things, upgrading things, numbers go up, get new blueprints, upgrade the upgrades. Rinse and repeat. And seeing the planet slowly revitalize with my actions is a really cool thing I've never seen in these kinds of games. It's not just aesthetic either, the growing plants reveal new resources and pathways. But after a while, the momentum starts to slow down, and once the oxygen is ubiquitous, the danger and planning is gone. This is where I lost steam, and I know there is quite a bit more of the game left, but it's like. . . just the game mechanics without any of the incentives. The end game is everything I've already done, but 1000x, build a 1000 of everything, 1000 more hours of picking up resources, automating it. The exploration and light mysteries/lore and basically done, there are no npc's, the game is really easy and tedious... It's a bit of a mixed bag. I'm certain the freedom and endgame will entice some, but without the right balance, it kinda just looses the magic. But I may try to jump back in after a break to see if the end game has any cool moments, or maybe playing with a friend? Half the tedium? (Or less with more friends)

But give it a try, I'm certain, if you like crafters, you'll mostly like this game and the very least.

I've put over 1000 hours into this game and i'm still finding things I haven't seen before (and not just because the developers added a TON of content with the 2.0 update).   Out of my catalog of games this is one of 3 that I play on a regular basis..  It's challenging without being stressful.  Easy to understand without being simplistic.  Great game to relax to at the end of the day or to loose an entire weekend playing.

The first 20-30 hours are pretty great, after that I felt like it was just waiting for the next phase. Probably would be much better with friends, but I'm 56 and my friends think I'm weird because I play video games, so there's that. But if they make a sequel and put a little more story into it, I'll be day one.
It's an interesting game, but it lacks depth and is significantly too grindy.

This game has a great idea but that's about it. The controls feel clunky, the building is imprecise or blocky, and worst of all, the game play loop is mundane. You run around, collect resources, bring it back to build a building that doesn't feel like it does anything. I really wanted to like this game, so Im sad to give it thubms down
Casual, great progression rate, not the micro-management detail necessary with other similar world-builders.
I think this game is quite relaxing but does get stale after a while as the new things to explore while you wait for stuff to generate isnt endless.

I tried the demo and prologue (both free) for this game with my friend, and we can both agree, we had a great time! I bought this game because me and my friend wanted to complete it, however.... he still needs to buy it... so imma get it for him! It runs pretty smooth on my computer so far, tried creative and did not even get laggy! My friend has a CPU bottleneck so it does not run too well but he can still play it, on low settings! 

Aside from the bad stuff (mainly device issues) I rate it 10/10 would play!

Its a nice fun game for those who like to explore, build and just chill, but problem is that it does not feel finished, there are bugs with planet vehicle, screen effects and other. After all its still a great game, i recommend trying it.

This game is absolutely brilliant! My favourite game at the moment, an excellent idea they had as well. I have attached my specs of my laptop that I am playing with right now (I have to play on low of course) but just to show how bad the specs can be for you to still enjoy this game. I sometimes switch over and play on my Desktop on Ultra but I still love it just the same on my laptop and haven't run into any issues. So happy that I can chill on the sofa with my cats and heater on with this game. It's winter and sitting at my desktop is NOT what I want to be doing most of the time. Can't believe I can enjoy a game like this on my potato laptop.
its an amazing and interactive game definitly worth it

Fun as hell time waster,
Solid world building and the steady growth of the planet as you play is very Cool.

I love this game if you couldn't tell from my 170 hours of game time. There's something about seeing a lifeless planet get literally terraformed by your own hand piece by piece that's incredibly satisfying.

Oh and the game has a plot and a story sort of in the background, but it's dumb and you should ignore it.

I've had a ton of fun with this game! It's very clearly made with love, and co-op multiplayer runs great (aside from the occasional bug, which usually ends up being more funny than bothersome).

It's such a unique and satisfying experience to watch a barren planet slowly come to life from all your efforts. I'll definitely be watching these devs to see what they do next!
Not a bad survival game, so far. It's grindy, which makes the play hard to balance, but I like it. Will definitely look into mods to extend the fun.
This is an awesome game with plenty to do. With the recent update I got back into the planet crafting sprit and want to have others share this feeling. 11 out of 10 game.

This game is very addictive! The sounds and music are terrific. I love that you can get all the endings without having to replay the game. Progression is very steady and I love the drone system as well. This really helped with not having so much monotony like most survival games. With almost 50 hours in this game I would totally play again and recommend to friends!!

Super chill game with a great vibe! love how it looks and feels. My only suggestion would be to improve the lighting of the game (some RT options maybe?) the atmosphere, illumination would be excellent. Hoping to see more of this game in the future
Despite my own computer's limitations, I thoroughly enjoy every minute I spend playing and terraforming. I enjoy the subtle differences in the DLC planets as well as the storyline.

worth the 10 bucks i spent on sale, only thing is i lost my appetite continuing the game when i terraformed the planet basically.. didnt have anything else todo expect now just get items and upgrade this and that. 
I suppose exploring made me love this game.
A great survival game that provides a great deal of satisfaction as you watch the world around you change.
i really enjoyed the adventure the game offers. starts off slow and painful but picks up mid to late game.

A really enjoyable game without any of the stressful / scary elements that I first expected might be involved with bringing a foreign planet back to life. There are some minor visual quirks but they barely affecct gameplay at all. It is super rewarding to see the effects all of your actions have on your environment and I enjoy that there have been more areas / features added overtime so that there is replayability. I definitely recommend the game for anyone who likes base building and space-themed games, there are endless possibilities for what you can create on your planet(s).
Extremely fun exploration with some challenge and no combat. Decent crafting and fun base building. Very satisfying upgrades. A bit janky physics, but it adds to the charm.
Lots of fun, the hitboxes on the cliffs are a bit weird, but overall good progression and storytelling

I thought this was gonna just be another No Man's Sky (...not the biggest fan of that game), so I never wanted to give it a chance but I finally saw the demo. Let me tell you... I have been ADDICTED to this game ever since I did the demo and purchased the full game! This is what I wish No Man's Sky was. And its literally half the price... Well worth it and such a fun game! And I haven't even purchased the DLCs... YET! ;)
Great game, cant wait to explore the DLC now that I am nearing the end of the base game!

давно хотела что-то такое с терраформированием и постройкой базы. и вот это оно.  крайне приятная вещь. виды неба в разное время суток и метеоритных дождях - отдельное произведение искуства. потупить в красоты космоса - крайне залипательно

I find the game very relaxing, nothing to fear exept your vitals.  If you enjoy basic building/construction and simple to more advanced orginization of recourses, you should find this game very enjoyable in 2026.
Great game! Realy love how it developed. I started play this game after played demo and watched some youtubers play demo to. Great work Developers!
Really amazing game, accidently bought this game mistaking it for another game and had a lot of fun completing it.
Peaceful terraforming. Love this game. Lot of hours spent. Relaxing to me compared to some other similar survival games. I love those too of course. Just need a "decompress" game too.
Awesome game. Lots to do and to keep you busy. Never a dull moment. Glad to see the devs continue to expand on the original with the game expansions and DLC's.
I love this game and regret not buying the bundle. If you like crafting and open world games you will love this game.
This game is super fun! Im the type that enjoys exploring and crafting, and this game is the best mix of the two. I highly suggest playing this game!
10/10. The perfect combo of grind and explore while maintaining a *relatively* stress-free game!
Great game for those wanting a "chill" building game to relax and enjoy.  Runs great on Linux via Proton.  Highly recommend.

Could've sworn I already reviewed this. It's a relatively simple game, a base-building game with elements of idle games like Cookie Clicker. I personally played through the game with a friend. I personally feel that if I had played it alone, I probably wouldn't have enjoyed it as much, as the game can get a bit repetitive, and sometimes may feel a bit slow. Either way, it's an alright time killer and pretty fun to screw around in, especially spamming the unstuck button and flying into space.

Just finished to terraform my very first planet and got into a second one, seems like I'm at almost 100% completion ( first time ever on a game tbh) 
The game is just awesome. I'm hoping one day it does get a huge graphic remaster , despite being already very pretty , i wish i could experience it all over again with photorealistic graphics, during my playthrough some of the flora made me think of the world of Avatar : Frontier of Pandora and i wish it would be a similar level of graphism. That being said , the world of Prime is tasteful , very pretty , well thought out, I enjoyed exploring every nook and cranny till the end. Right now i just started the terraforming on a new planet HUMBLE and what i love about being able to not start over from scratch is that it's giving a different gameplay so far : instead of creating , im kinda reverse engeneering what i find on the map for the moment to get the ressources so its quite fun. Hoping this game keeps coming with content and improvements cause im officially addicted. 
ONE LAST THING/ PROBLEM : Since the 70 hour mark , the game has been crashing to desktop every hour or so for some reason, so i dont know whats this all about ??

I don't usually enjoy survival/crafting games, but this one completely enraptured me. The way that the environment transforms as you work to terraform the planets is magical, and some of the scenery is genuinely breathtaking. There is a good amount of mechanical depth too, some nice exploration and a bit of nice storytelling, though nothing to write home about.  

I wish the game leaned more into narrative content, or automation gameplay, because both feel fairly basic and undercooked, but the game is not really about either so it's not a big deal in my opinion.
Great game, friendly to new players. P.S don't build in the starter area, thank me later.

I bought this game at full price, and I do not regret it.

A survival craft dressed up with a ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ tuxedo, holy smokes this game is good. Such a simple game, so easy to play, except the mechanics are all controlled either from simple, easy to read menus, or through real-time, physical machines that we create. Resource gathering plays on human's natural ability to recognize and remember different shapes and colors without the need for poopy highlight outlines telling you everything, the HUD is minimalistic in the best possible way, the exploration mechanics like discovering your world's cardinal directions, creating an overworld map, maybe eventually even getting a minimap, even traversal through the world- everything is paced out so perfectly from one another while allowing the player to naturally discover and explore on their own.

And the depth! This isn't some shallow pond spread thin over a large map, this game is an ocean!

The moment I understood how deep this game goes, the moment I became aware of its magnitude, all whilst, mind you, still steadily maintaining mechanical progression, still seeing how much left I have to go, I was staggered. Literally my jaw dropped an inch. Everything is interconnected. Every aspect of the survival mechanics play an important role all the way into mid to late game. We're 16.9 hours in at the time of writing this, after only 2 play sessions mind you, we both had days off and both of us just didn't want to stop lol, and me and my gf are just now hitting mid game. The pacing is literally perfect. It's a little faster with two people, in a good way, but it's only faster because we're both able to gather and build at the same time.

And there are seasons??!?!!?!? I think?

Except one season is just ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥- Meteor swarms??!?!? I didn't even pick up on it until a few cycles in, once my base started progressing into a smooth living rhythm- there's a wet season, a dry one, and a dry one with meteor swarms constantly bombarding the continent. I'm pretty sure. I could be wrong, but I'm pretty sure. It all may just have to do with our terraformation progression, but just the fact that it has me guessing at all is so cool. If there aren't seasons, the devs should definitely think about adding them.

I will say, a minor and petty gripe I have is with the indoor ladders, but I'm realizing now that that may just be an early-game quirk. They can't be stacked. It's not a big deal, but if I make a tower, there isn't a cubicle early in that would essentially allow for or create a ladderwell, and we can't stack the ladders, so it ends up being a game of clock, spinning back and forth going up and down disorientatingly depending on how you position them. Windows definitely alleviate some of the disorient, but not completely. I may have done that to my self. I mean, that in and of itself is kind of fun, and I like how it essentially created a naturally emerging tower design, but having a half-empty, half-grate cubicle that you could seamlessly attach a ladder to would be so good. So so good. And if we could stack ladders, we could click on the top or bottom half of it to go up or down. But I guess for now we're dealing with the issue by just spinning clockwise over and over again, or keeping the ladders next to each other. Again it's just a QoL thing that would be awesome to have. Hell, maybe it already exists and we just haven't unlocked it yet. I wouldn't be surprised if that were the case, again the game is ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ massive and I could see the devs having the foresight.

Anyways, the game is top tier. There's subtle environmental storytelling all over the dang place and the map just keeps slowly opening up wider and wider. Our perspective keeps expanding. And the physicality of the game is so, so good. Interacting with the systems we create, not just through a menu but seeing it happen in the world is such a nice touch. The transformation of the planet is in real-time, and I remember because of that having spent like 5 minutes talking with my gf over where the lake is probably going to form because of the shape of the geography, and at what water level it's more than likely going to plateau at. It's just so good.

The resource scavenging loop is sped up progressively and satisfyingly with the tech upgrades, and automating production becomes natural as we progress further. Upgrades are exponential, and there are even a few multiplicative ones. We've already built a few different buildings that are essentially work-horses as well, which frees up much needed time doing more important progression tasks.

I could go on, to be honest, but these are the biggest things I wanted to mention. The game is good. Buy it. 2 years into its release and it's at Overwhelmingly Positive, and I completely understand why.

There are some games you get hooked on instantly.

There are games where doing good, like terraforming a planet to make it habitable, really works.

You arrive on this planet, you try to survive while making it habitable.

Then you see it change, life returns, and all thanks to your actions.

Playing this game made me feel good. Feeling useful, even virtually, has its advantages.

[spoiler]All achievements / 10[/spoiler]

[spoiler]Thank you for this game[/spoiler]
Really satisfying gameplay loop and interplanetary travel.  Replay value is kind of low but from a team of 2 this is pretty fantastic.

65 Hours to finish the base game. Granted a lot of that was exploration, learning curve, etc.; but I played it practically continuous for less than 2 weeks.

This game has scratched an itch I didn't even know I had. It needs a bit of polish, but I'm *really* happy that, as a new player, the devs appear to have prioritized new content release and expansion. I say that because this game has been so addictive and engaging that I would be *extremely* disappointed if my progress to this point would be all there was. I say that as an absolute positive.

I love survival-crafting games, but I should point out that I played on "relaxed" mode, and I'm glad I did: my character died *constantly* from starvation and/or dehydration, mostly because there are so many things to think about and do (definitely not a bad thing) that it's really easy to forget to pack food.  However, progress in this game happens exponentially.  The time to the next convenience/QoL/Unlock happens faster decreases with each step. 

10/10 Excellent survival-crafting game
Very addictive survival, basebuilding, and resource management, with little to no death punishment. The loop doesn't get tiring, you can play for hours on end. Enough content for 100+ hours.

Took me this long to finish the game. And it was a pleasure cruise. At first i thought that it would be a snooze fest without combat, but the developers hit the bullseye with exploration. I was compelled to explrore and i enjoed it. The building was nice, varied enough but not too tedious.
The most interesting thing is that you pace the game your self. The pace of tech unlocks is as fast or slow as you like.
I wholeheartedly recommend this game to anyone wanting a similar game to Subnautica, to anyone looking for a survival game with nice progression and base building.

Most of the negative I've seen appear to be pre-release complaints.  The most recent patch addresses pretty much everything.  This is a clean, polished, final product.  It's very casual, offers lots of exploration and building.  There's plenty of end-game content and stretch goals for those who are intrinsically motivated.  Managing hunger and thirst late-game becomes somewhat tedious though.  Overall very enjoyable and replayable.
once u understand the mechanics of terraforming, u ll start loving the game as the progression is just so well paced. this is about 30 mins into the game ish
I love this game because of all the things you can build and make.   I would recommend anyone that loves to build and make new things to play this game.

I really enjoyed The Planet Crafter. It is one of those games where you start with almost nothing and, without even realising it, you end up completely hooked because every small upgrade feels useful.

What I like the most is the sense of progression. You are not just collecting resources and building random things. You can actually see the planet changing because of what you do, and that makes the game feel very rewarding. Going from a dead, empty planet to a place with water, plants and life is honestly a great feeling.

The game is relaxing, but still gives you plenty to do. There is always another upgrade to unlock, another area to explore, or something better to build in your base. I also like that it focuses more on exploration, crafting and terraforming instead of combat, which makes it a nice change from other survival games.

It is not perfect, but it has a lot of charm and a very addictive gameplay loop. If you enjoy survival crafting games, base building, exploration, or just watching your progress slowly transform the world around you, The Planet Crafter is definitely worth playing.

Highly recommended.

If you like survival crafting games this title will scratch that ich. exploration wins you crafting recipes and such. good for chilling out. or ramp up the difficult to make a bit more intense. your game play it your way.

Very nice game. One of the few I actually completed. If you expect something like Satisfactory, you will be disappointed. On the other hand, if you'd want to like Satisfactory but it's too tedious to recreate the same machines again and again, this here might be for you.

Setting up energy for the base is as simple as building one machine and instantly using the power on the whole planet. It's not realistic, it's fun. You need water, put down a water collector and it collects the water and makes it into bottles of water. You don't have to mine glass and put it into the collector to receive water.

Exploration is always rewarding, the contents of the chests you find are updated based on your current terraformation level, so the longer you play, the better the items. You can't accidentally loot an endgame area with all the good stuff at the beginning of the game. There are a few options of traversal unlocking throughout the game. Storage is a tiny bit clunky, but isn't it always? It get's better, though.

In general it's a nice game that was fun until the end.

Planet Crafter is really a good peaceful teraformation game developed by a small team.
You land on a desert planet and slowly bring life to it by producing oxygen, pressure, heat and biomass.
There are many places to discover with new items to loot which give you access to better machines, plants, and animals. It is really pleasant to see your actions having an impact on the planet development, (you can choose which trees will grow around your base). You see the sky becoming blue, rain starting to fall and lakes starting to fill.
The only bad point of this game is the graphics which are not very beautiful, especially the textures.

What an amazingly consistent and positive game!

You land on a hostile Mars-like desert. You slowly build it up into a pleasant garden.

The DLC adds worlds with their own interesting secrets and playstyles. Excellent game!
fantastic game. like subnautica but no water or creatures. the environment is exciting and dangerous.

is a good game, i went for the all the achievements but it dont let me have the golden flower one even tho i grab a ton of those. at this point i dont know what to do to be honest do i need to start over?

Hell yes I recommend it.  

It definitely has more of a 'sandboxy' feel to it and things perhaps are a bit more basic as far as design.  For example, You just have to slap down heat/power generators on SOME part of the map and BOOM, you have available power.   There's no fiddling with running wires from your generator to your facility.  If you are demanding more 'realism' as far as wiring power and placing things in your base, this game is not that sophisticated, at least not that I've discovered.

That said, this game does have mechanics that allow you to build drones that can supply your various storages.  When I figured out how to use them, I just absolutely love watching those little drones swoop in and either take or supply stuff.  

There's a bit of a 'sloggy' feel to accumulating resources but that's to be expected of any crafting game.  

Graphically beautiful and I love that they didn't get anal about your rover crashing into things or being obstructed by the smallest obstacles.  Also being able to teleport with ores and materials is beautiful (are you hearing me Valheim?).

Games like this are to be applauded for knowing what the core audience loves in crafting games.  It's definitely worth your patronage.  Sorry Subnautica 2.  I bought you but you're going to have to wait.
It's a really fun and relaxing game. One time i spent 6 hours and didn't even realize because i was having too much fun.
Super chill, steady progression, that i can play at my own pace wether its solo or with friends
This game is good for a boring day and sparks tons of creativity I highly recamend
Long hours of building, exploring, terraforming. No real threats or fighting. Just great for relaxing after work.

Planet Crafter is a good game or it would be a great game if the following ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ are addressed.
1. Save games options are virtually non existent.
2. Can crash for no obvious reasons. No helpful error log.
3. Save games corrupted, again no obvious reason.

The PC is up to date i.e. Bios, GPU drivers, OS and the game is verified after each crash.
The game can run for hours without crashing.

its okay game. but sometimes the grafic suck and lag. if you build toooo much like i did. then game not crashes but laags as crazy. but hopfuly thet will fix it

Tried this out after finishing the Subnautica 2 EA, and I was blown away by how fun this game is. The base-building, exploration, and automation all work so well and give you plenty of options to play how you want. This is honestly one of the best games I've ever played.

This game has a lot of the same vibes as the original Subnautica, in all the best ways. It has a fascinating bit of lore, and even more potential because it's inherently a game about terraforming some planet you've been sent to. There's a degree to which you even have a certain amount of control over determining the path.

The one thing I WOULD ask for is, then, more control. The downside of the genetic elements of the game is a lack of versatility and manipulability. That's understandable, because it's a game and it can't be unlimited. Even still, I'd like to see a few dozen more plant & animal options introduced, in one way or another.
It's a fun game that is a good game for all ages! Im kinda sad that there isn't any fighting, but I like it anyway!!!

I love this game! It's so fun! I grabbed it because of the YouTuber ThatFoolishGrrl and I'm so glad I did. I haven't gotten super far into the game despite how long I've played, but that's because I'm learning and exploring. I really do love it, and I look forward to more updates and potentially new planets!
loving this game, really dont have anything bad to say.  Get it play it and have fun
This game is super satisfying, I love how the planets change over the course of the game.

I have several hundred hours in this game. I love and adore it. It scratches multiple itches for me - engineering, exploration, environmental cleanup. I especially love how the environmental cleanup was expanded upon in the Toxicity DLC. I hope these developers continue to pump out new worlds for us to terraform and keep giving us new ways to beautify and heal broken lands.

I had this sitting in my library for a while but never got around to playing it. I just finished a play-through of the base game and if you like survival/base building games, this is a great one. Some of the systems don't stack up to more of the newer titles, but it is still amazing. The game rewards you for exploring every corner of the planet. Watching the transformation from when you start to how you finish is really awesome. I'd highly recommend giving it a try!
Great game, enjoyed it a lot. It is cool that the environment that you are playing in changes so much based on your decisions.
good game love the concept just wish for some alien or creature interactions

A great survival game. It includes lots of exploration, crafting, base building and late game automation. The story and the graphics are a little weak, but the gameplay more than makes up for it. There is a mid-game hump to get over. Once you have the necessary tech though, it become a real pleasure. Some free DLC is a plus too. Highly reccomended

The visuals aren't great but it really doesn't matter because the gameplay is SO GOOD. 

BUY THIS GAME NOW.

Not sure if it counts as a "cozy" game, but for sure a comfort game for me. Fun planet terra-former, good pace and the new things to build come quickly. The DLCs and updates have added a lot of replayability even for an early adopter like me.

Great game... very detailed. I would ask the developers to study another game called Empyrion to see how base building is better in a lot of ways. Maybe if you add an ELEVATOR, just a thought. Trying to build and connect the buildings is just a little wonky.... Also, plz consider introducing a choice of cubes that all ready have windows! When it comes to producing large #s of anything  IE = fuses, could you consider a choice of X25 and or X10 .... I create a boatload of fuses and it = a click per one! I must have 10,000 clicks for fuses alone.
if you like survival/crafting/automation games, but want a clear goal to work towards, you'll probably really like this game. Not as complicated as Satisfactory either.

This game is extremely fun! I love the visual progress as you terraform the planet makes it so much more satisfying! I can honestly say im addicted and can’t stop playing!!!! Now back to terraformation bye!
Great game, highly recommended. My inly advice - minimize using external help, this make the game so much more satisfying and joyful!

Solid game, interesting progression, good "small loop" and especially "big loop" that lets you use what you learned on new planets from scratch, with twists. Some minor annoyances (the game uses proper SI units and prefixes, but still types out stupid things like 10 000 000 nano, instead of adjusting to 10 milli like you should - that's just... come on), and I would personally do some tuning here and there, but nothing game-breaking, far from it. The devs have kept working on it and just released the 2.0 update that added new and updated stuff, for free. This fact alone is above and beyond and worth a recommendation.

This game is amazing! I spent the first 160 hours playing solo but recently started a multiplayer game with my daughter and we've had a blast. The devs have done an awesome job on the game and kept it interesting all the way through. It is a very chill game and one that I will keep coming back to.
